subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Tunguska: The Mystery Explosion
description: A fast breakdown of Tunguska: The Mystery Explosion for builders.

hook.text: In 1908, a massive explosion flattened 80 million trees across 2,000 km² of Siberia. No crater was found.

buildup.text: The blast was 1,000 times more powerful than Hiroshima. Windows shattered hundreds of km away.

discovery.text: A small asteroid or comet fragment likely exploded in the atmosphere before ever reaching the ground.

twist.text: Nobody investigated for 19 years because the area was so remote. The forest was already regrowing.

climax.text: If it had arrived 4 hours later, Earth's rotation would have placed St. Petersburg underneath.

punchline.text: No crater, no fragments. Just 80 million flattened trees.

quiz.question: Why was no crater found at the Tunguska explosion site?
options: The object exploded in the atmosphere before hitting the ground
The crater was filled in by volcanic activity shortly after
The explosion occurred over a lake that absorbed the impact
